findings: The hook uses a concrete two-gym cost comparison that gives students an immediate reason to care about a system of equations before any algebra appears.
The concept slide explicitly defines a solution as an ordered pair that makes BOTH equations true at the same time, addressing the most common 'I only checked one' mistake.
Both pause problems are solvable from material already shown, and each is followed by a visually distinct answer-reveal slide with full algebra, not just the final ordered pair.
The 'multiply every term, not just one' trap slide targets a real elimination mistake students make, with a side-by-side wrong / right comparison.
The closing path slide gives students concrete Learn Portal next actions (OpenStax reading, Khan Academy practice, dashboard assignment, advisor check-in) rather than generic encouragement.
watch_points: Slide 10 packs three sub-cases (one / none / infinite) into one frame; narration must move slowly here and the contact-sheet thumbnail should be re-checked at small size.
Students who confuse 'no solution' with 'zero is a solution' may need an advisor follow-up before Module 12.
